title: Hiyori and Yasuie Reveal Wano’s Pain
description: On pages 7–10, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6, Ashura Doji, Unknown person #7, Zoro, Unknown person #8 as Hiyori’s identity and Yasuie’s sacrifice pull the Wano rebellion into a deeper emotional crisis.
scene: Zoro protects Toko and meets Hiyori, while Yasuie prepares a sacrifice meant to rescue the rebellion’s plan.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: How much longer for the fight- ing?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oi! How'd you two get so fat overnight?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Master Luffy, I told you they'd find out if you overate!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Find out?! With some- thing like that, they'd have to be idiots to not notice!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How has an idiot like that survived this long?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Should we report that all his Oshiruko somehow vanished overnight?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Of course not, dumbo! Who knows what he'll do if he gets two bits of bad news at once!

dialogue.text: ARSON?! Things have gotten more out of hand than I had imagined.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I just hope it pushes him to become an enemy of the hundred beast pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Idiots like him don't move unless they are pushed!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Our plans have derailed and I see no way to get them back on track!

dialogue.text: Our plans have derailed and I see no way to get them back on track!

dialogue.text: You just had to get cocky and steal food and weapons, huh, head mountain bandits?!

summary: This section advances the Wano - Hiyori and Yasuie storyline with 41 dialogue beats. Hiyori and Yasuie bring the rebellion’s emotional history into the foreground.
